subject: our 3-year announcement
body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

Today marks a special day for me...

Three years ago, I started Morningside AI.

Back then, it was just me in my room building basic chatbots and wondering whether any of this would actually turn into something real...

Fast forward to today and we are a 55+ person team across Morningside AI and the Accelerator, helping some of the world biggest companies actually deploy AI in real-world environments.

So to celebrate the milestone, I wanted to do something a little different...

Last week I messaged my team of engineers and coaches and asked them one question:

What is your one secret for building a successful AI Agency in 2026?

They came back with these 5 tips:

Tip 1 - You do not have to be a developer
The biggest myth in the AI space is that you have to be a developer to run an AI Agency. My business partner Josh runs operations at Morningside. He has never written a line of code in his life, yet he is helping run a world-class AI agency working with massive clients. Businesses care about the outcome, not the technical explanation.

Tip 2 - Think Bigger!
Most people think AI agency deals cap out at 5k, 10k, maybe 15k. Reality check: Our Accelerator members average 32k per deal. Morningside closed multiple 500k+ projects this year alone. If the leverage is big, the budgets are big.

Tip 3 - Development is only a fraction
About 30% of a project is building the system. About 70% is optimisation, adoption, and change-management. If AI projects fail, it is usually because this part gets ignored.

Tip 4 - Get out from behind that screen!
An online presence helps, but some of the biggest wins still happen in person. Three of our largest deals last year came from face-to-face relationships.

Tip 5 - You do not need a huge amount of capital
Josh used Claude Code to scope an entire multi-six-figure project. When one of our engineers reviewed it later, it was 95% aligned with their own scoping. A non-technical founder can pitch the solution, validate feasibility with AI, collect a deposit, then hire their first developer after the deal closes.

The blunt reality of starting an AI business:

Anyone can build an AI agency. But most people simply will not. They will over-research. They will tool-hop. They will try to figure everything out at once and burn out.

The opportunity is not slowing down. But it does reward the people who stop waiting and start moving.

Keep going,

Liam
